perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Permitted Purpose ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Authorized Use ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Authorized Use for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Permitted Purpose' to 'Authorized Use' creates confusion. Later sections refer to 'Permitted Purpose.' This change creates uncertainty whether those sections now refer to 'Authorized Use' instead, or if Developer can rely on any definition of 'Permitted Purpose' at all. This is contradictory, since the contract uses both phrases without clearly defining if they are to be treated as one and the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data a. Non-Use .Developer shall not use any Sample Data for any purpose other than the Permitted Purpose. b. Non-Disclosure .Except as provided in subsection (c) below, Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Purpose. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement. Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information. Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation. Developer shall not reproduce any Sample Data in any form or store it in a retrieval system or database without FDI’s prior written consent, except as Developer reasonably may require for the Permitted Purpose. c. Legal Compulsion .If Developer becomes legally compelled to disclose any Sample Data, Developer shall provide FDI with prompt prior written notice of such requirement so that FDI may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy, or both, or waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Permitted Purpose' to 'Authorized Use' in the definition section directly contradicts the 'Non-Disclosure and Non-Use of Sample Data' section, which explicitly refers to 'Permitted Purpose'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contract now uses two different terms for what seems to be the same concept without clarifying if they are interchangeable, leading to confusion about which term controls the use of Sample Data and potentially causing disputes over contractual obligations. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Purpose. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a nondisclosure agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Scope. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a confidentiality agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'nondisclosure agreement' to 'confidentiality agreement' and Permitted Purpose to Permitted Scope introduces inconsistent terminology. The document does not define a 'Permitted Scope', so it is contradictory with the original 'Permitted Purpose', also in the rest of the text are referencing to the initial naming convention 'nondisclosure agreement'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: b. Non-Disclosure .Except as provided in subsection (c) below, Developer shall not disclose any Sample Data to any third party or to its own employees, agents or professional advisors except employees, agents or professional advisors who are required to have the Sample Data to implement the Permitted Scope. Developer shall have each employee to whom any Sample Data will be disclosed, or who will have access to Sample Data, sign a confidentiality agreement that is substantially similar to this Agreement. Developer shall take all reasonable measures to protect the secrecy of and avoid disclosure or use of the Sample Data, including, without limitation, using the highest degree of care that Developer uses to protect its own confidential information. Developer shall notify FDI in writing of any misuse or misappropriation of Sample Data immediately after it becomes aware of such misuse or misappropriation. Developer shall not reproduce any Sample Data in any form or store it in a retrieval system or database without FDI’s prior written consent, except as Developer reasonably may require for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The change introduces 'Permitted Scope' and 'confidentiality agreement', but the contradicted text refers to 'Permitted Purpose' and 'nondisclosure agreement'. This creates a direct contradiction because the terms are used in similar contexts and appear to be meant to reference the same thing, leading to ambiguity and inconsistency as defined by Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction. The explanation states that the document doesn't define 'Permitted Scope', but 'Permitted Purpose' exists. The original text also states 'nondisclosure agreement'. The contradicted text also has 'Permitted Scope' and 'confidentiality agreement' which is a change.

perturbation.original_text: When the Assessment Period is over, if the parties have not agreed to go forward and sign a comprehensive agreement that permits Developer to use the Data to modify its Applications and pursuant to which FDI will provide Developer with the technical documents that are necessary for Developer to do so, Developer shall return to FDI or destroy all copies of the Sample Data in its possession or control.
perturbation.changed_text: When the Evaluation Term is over, if the parties have not agreed to go forward and sign a comprehensive agreement that permits Developer to use the Data to modify its Applications and pursuant to which FDI will provide Developer with the technical documents that are necessary for Developer to do so, Developer shall return to FDI or destroy all copies of the Sample Data in its possession or control.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Assessment Period' to 'Evaluation Term' introduces inconsistent terminology that is not defined, this can lead to legal dispute on the meaning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Permitted Purpose ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below
perturbation.justification: The original text used the term "Assessment Period", which is a defined term in the "Contradicted Text". The changed text replaces this with "Evaluation Term", which is not defined. This creates a misalignment in terminology as the contract now uses an undefined term where a defined term previously existed, causing ambiguity and potentially leading to disputes about which period the clause refers to. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, as it uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Due to the valuable and proprietary nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory.
perturbation.changed_text: Due to the valuable and confidential nature of the Sample Data, the obligations assumed by Developer hereunder other than those stated in Section 1(c) shall be unlimited in time or territory.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'proprietary nature' to 'confidential nature' changes the scope of what is being protected. 'Proprietary' suggests ownership rights, while 'confidential' suggests secrecy. This is conflicting, because FDI is stating in the paragraph above and throughout the text, the valuable and proprietary nature of the data, not confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Ownership .Developer acknowledges that FDI owns the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s ownership of the Data or the Sample Data.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the 'valuable and proprietary nature' of the Sample Data, while the changed text refers to the 'valuable and confidential nature'. The contradicted text states 'FDI owns the Sample Data'. Changing proprietary to confidential weakens FDI's claim to ownership, creating a contradiction. Proprietary implies ownership, which aligns with the contradicted section. Confidential implies secrecy, which does not necessarily imply ownership and weakens or contradicts FDI's ownership as stated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Developer acknowledges that FDI owns the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s ownership of the Data or the Sample Data.
perturbation.changed_text: Developer acknowledges that FDI possesses the Sample Data and agrees not to challenge FDI’s possession of the Data or the Sample Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'owns' to 'possesses' significantly weakens FDI's claim. 'Owns' implies full legal ownership, while 'possesses' merely implies holding it. This creates a contradiction with Section 5, which relies on FDI's ownership for injunctive relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Developer agrees that money damages would not be an adequate remedy for unauthorized use or disclosure by it of any Sample Data, and that, in addition to all other remedies, Developer shall be entitled to specific performance and injunctive or other equitable relief as a remedy for Developer’s unauthorized use or disclosure, without the requirement of posting any bond. In the event that FDI deems it appropriate to initiate any action to enforce the obligations of Developer under this Agreement, and should Developer be held to have breached this Agreement, Developer shall reimburse FDI for all costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’ fees.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that FDI 'owns' the Sample Data. The changed text modifies this to 'possesses'. The explanation states that this weakens FDI's claim as 'owns' implies full legal ownership, while 'possesses' only implies holding it. Section 5 discusses injunctive relief, which relies on FDI's ownership of the data. Changing 'owns' to 'possesses' directly contradicts the basis for injunctive relief, making enforcement unclear, thus a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions.
perturbation.changed_text: This Contract shall be governed by the laws of the State of New York without regard to its conflicts of laws provisions.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' in the governing law clause, while still using 'Agreement' everywhere else, introduces unnecessary ambiguity. This creates an in-text contradiction because it is unclear if the entire NDA is now a 'Contract' or just the governing law part. If just the governing law part, it is contradictory on what laws are to be imposed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT This Non-Disclosure Agreement is entered into by and between Frames Data Inc., a California corporation with a business address at 100 Avenue of the Americas, New York, New York 10013 (“FDI ”), and ________________________________, a ______________ __________________ with a business address at __________________ (“Developer ”). WHEREAS, FDI has compiled and maintains certain databases pertaining to ophthalmic frame style specifications which it updates and modifies from time to time that includes textual and graphic data relating to frames manufactured by various manufacturers (the “Data ”); Developer develops optical practice management software applications (the “Applications ”) and wishes to obtain a sample of certain Data (the “Sample Data ”) for the sole purpose of assessing whether Developer can adapt the Applications to be compatible with the Sample Data (the “Permitted Purpose ”); and FDI is willing to provide Developer with a sample of Sample Data for the Permitted Purpose for a limited time (the “Assessment Period ”) on the terms and conditions set forth below
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Contract' introduces ambiguity regarding the governing law. While the document is clearly labeled as a 'NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT', changing 'Agreement' to 'Contract' within the governing law clause creates confusion. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition, as using 'Agreement' and 'Contract' interchangeably without clear definition can lead to conflicting interpretations. The contradicted text refers to the document as an 'Agreement', and the change could suggest that only the governing law portion is a 'Contract', leading to uncertainty about which law governs the entire NDA.
